questions.q: What is the Kshira Sagara in Hindu cosmology?
questions.explanation: In the Puranas the Kshira Sagara is the primordial ocean of milk upon which Vishnu reclines.

questions.q: Which deity is described as reclining upon the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: The Vishnu and Bhagavata Puranas describe Vishnu reclining upon the ocean of milk in his cosmic repose.

questions.q: Upon which serpent does Vishnu recline on the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: Vishnu rests upon the great serpent Shesha, also called Ananta, on the ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which goddess attends Vishnu as he reclines upon the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe the goddess Lakshmi attending Vishnu upon the ocean of milk.

questions.q: The famous episode of churning the Kshira Sagara is best known by which name?
questions.explanation: The churning of the ocean of milk is known as the Samudra Manthana.

questions.q: What did the devas and asuras seek to obtain by churning the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: The devas and asuras churned the ocean of milk to obtain amrita, the nectar of immortality.

questions.q: Which goddess is said in the Puranas to have arisen from the churning of the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: The goddess Lakshmi arose from the ocean of milk during the Samudra Manthana.

questions.q: The Kshira Sagara is most literally translated as which of the following?
questions.explanation: The Sanskrit 'Kshira Sagara' means the ocean (sagara) of milk (kshira).

questions.q: In which two Puranas is Vishnu's repose upon the Kshira Sagara prominently described?
questions.explanation: The Vishnu and Bhagavata Puranas describe Vishnu reclining upon the ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which divine physician emerged from the Kshira Sagara during its churning?
questions.explanation: The physician of the gods, Dhanvantari, arose from the churning of the ocean of milk bearing the amrita.

questions.q: Which serpent was used as the rope to churn the Kshira Sagara in the Samudra Manthana?
questions.explanation: The serpent Vasuki served as the churning-rope coiled around the mountain during the Samudra Manthana.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, which mountain was used as the churning-rod for the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: Mount Mandara was used as the churning-rod in the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: As which avatar did Vishnu support Mount Mandara during the churning of the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the form of Kurma, the tortoise, to support the sinking Mount Mandara upon his back.

questions.q: Vishnu's yogic slumber upon the Kshira Sagara is known by which term?
questions.explanation: Vishnu's cosmic slumber upon the serpent Shesha on the ocean of milk is called yoga nidra.

questions.q: Which deadly poison emerged from the Kshira Sagara during its churning?
questions.explanation: The terrible poison Halahala emerged from the ocean of milk and threatened all creation.

questions.q: Which deity drank the Halahala poison that arose from the Kshira Sagara to save the cosmos?
questions.explanation: Shiva drank the Halahala poison, holding it in his throat, which turned blue and earned him the name Nilakantha.

questions.q: Which form did Vishnu assume to distribute the amrita and deceive the asuras after the churning of the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: Vishnu took the enchanting female form of Mohini to recover the amrita and distribute it to the devas.

questions.q: Which celestial elephant is said in the Puranas to have arisen from the churning of the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: The white elephant Airavata, mount of Indra, emerged from the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: Which divine horse is said to have emerged from the churning of the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: The radiant horse Uchchaihshravas arose from the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: The form of Vishnu who reclines in cosmic repose upon the Kshira Sagara is most often called by which name?
questions.explanation: Vishnu reclining upon the serpent Ananta on the ocean of milk is called Anantashayana or Sheshashayi Vishnu.

questions.q: In Puranic cosmology, the Kshira Sagara is counted among the great ringed oceans encircling which features?
questions.explanation: Puranic cosmology describes seven concentric oceans, including the ocean of milk, encircling the seven island-continents or dvipas.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, the wish-fulfilling tree Kalpavriksha and which divine cow arose from the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: The wish-granting cow Kamadhenu, also called Surabhi, emerged from the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: In the Bhagavata Purana account of the Samudra Manthana, who held the head of the serpent Vasuki while churning the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: In the Bhagavata Purana the asuras insisted on holding the head of Vasuki, while the devas held the tail.

questions.q: Which seductive celestial maidens (apsaras) are said to have arisen from the Kshira Sagara during its churning?
questions.explanation: The Puranas relate that lovely apsaras, including Rambha, emerged from the churning of the ocean of milk.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the moon (Chandra) is also numbered among the treasures said to arise from the churning of the Kshira Sagara, and Shiva is said to have placed it where?
questions.explanation: The moon arose during the churning, and Shiva bears the crescent moon within his matted locks.

questions.q: Which asura disguised himself among the devas to steal a share of the amrita drawn from the Kshira Sagara, only to be beheaded by Vishnu?
questions.explanation: The asura Svarbhanu, disguised among the gods, drank the amrita and was beheaded, becoming Rahu and Ketu.

questions.q: Which goddess of misfortune and elder sister of Lakshmi is also said in some Puranic accounts to have emerged from the churning of the Kshira Sagara?
questions.explanation: Some Puranic accounts relate that Jyestha, also called Alakshmi, the goddess of misfortune, arose from the ocean before Lakshmi.

questions.q: From whose navel, while reclining upon the Kshira Sagara, does Brahma emerge upon a lotus to begin creation in the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe Brahma emerging on a lotus from the navel of Vishnu as he reclines upon the ocean of milk.

questions.q: In Puranic cosmology, the Kshira Sagara (ocean of milk) surrounds which island-continent (dvipa)?
questions.explanation: In the Puranic scheme of seven concentric oceans, the ocean of milk encircles Shakadvipa, lying beyond the inner oceans of salt, sugarcane juice, wine, clarified butter, and curds.

questions.q: When the gods, distressed, sought refuge from a crisis, the Puranas describe them praying to Vishnu at the Kshira Sagara in a hymn known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Puranas record the gods praising Vishnu upon the ocean of milk in a hymn known as the Kshira Sagara stuti.
